text:
There once was a wise old owl named Old Featherfoot. Every morning she would flap her great wings and fly out to explore the world.

On a particularly foggy morning, Old Featherfoot flew along a riverbank when she noticed a small, tiny house. Curious, she flew closer.

There stood a little man, no bigger than a robin. He peered out at her.

"You there!" he exclaimed. "Come in here! I've got cookies!"

She looked around, perplexed.

"No, no," he said with a smile. "That's just my owl bed!"

Old Featherfoot landed softly beside him. "Oh?"

"Yep," he chuckled. "Soak up the sun!"

She looked around at his tiny garden. "You don't eat owl cookies?"

"We're ducks!" he confessed.

"It tastes like waterweed."

"Oh, ducks!" he laughed fondly.

Time passed. He showed her flowers, bugs, even some tiny fish. They became fast friends
